text: Overtraining syndrome is a maladapted response to excessive exercise without adequate rest, resulting in perturbations of multiple body systems including neurologic, endocrinologic, and immunologic systems coupled with mood changes.
Overtraining syndrome remains a clinical diagnosis with no definitive diagnostic test; history and limited serologies are the primary diagnostic tools.

Overreaching and overtraining syndrome exist on a continuum: most research has studied overreaching rather than true overtraining syndrome, meaning the conditions may differ in mechanism and recovery timeline.
Prevention of overtraining syndrome requires adequate rest relative to training load, as the syndrome results from excessive exercise without sufficient recovery.

Fatigue and unexplained underperformance in athletes should prompt evaluation for overtraining syndrome, which involves assessment of neurologic, endocrinologic, and immunologic perturbations as well as mood changes.
